acceptance_targets: [chapter_15_prose_001] Pass if the narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:399 reads as grammatically complete sentences with explicit subjects and verbs while preserving the same action. Fail if standalone narration fragments equivalent to "Not a poster. Not a slogan. A board." remain in those beats.

[chapter_15_craft_001] Pass if the revised text in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:91 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:129 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:225 includes enough Silas thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment that no run of 10 consecutive paragraphs remains purely external. Fail if either block still reads as uninterrupted external description and dialogue for 10 or more paragraphs.

[chapter_15_craft_002] Pass if at least two of the abstract glosses at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:405 are replaced by materially specific action or dialogue, and the shift from single authorship to shared infrastructure is still clear from the trays through the board sketch. Fail if the revised passage still explains the same single-name-versus-plural-use lesson in multiple abstract paraphrases after the scene has already shown it.
[FBR-010] Pass if both cited lines are rewritten so the chapter trusts dialogue, labor rhythm, and object handling to signal the realization; fail if either moment still uses a self-announcing hinge.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:399 reads as grammatically complete sentences with explicit subjects and verbs while preserving the same action. Fail if standalone narration fragments equivalent to "Not a poster. Not a slogan. A board." remain in those beats.
findings.evidence: The narration slips into clipped fragment chains at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:399. The most visible instance is the climax's "Not a poster. Not a slogan. A board. Several boards." construction, which violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.problem: The chapter's labor montage and realization beat rely on fragment-stacking instead of grammatically complete narration. That breaks the stated constitutional rule against clipped narration and makes the pressure feel mechanically punchy rather than fully inhabited.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:399 into complete sentences with explicit subjects and verbs. Keep the compression, but anchor it in Silas's body, the tray work, and the plank instead of bare fragment chains.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised text in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:91 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:129 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:225 includes enough Silas thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment that no run of 10 consecutive paragraphs remains purely external. Fail if either block still reads as uninterrupted external description and dialogue for 10 or more paragraphs.
findings.evidence: From snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:91 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:129, and again from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:225, the chapter runs through long strings of external description and dialogue before returning to explicit Silas interiority at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:131 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:227.
findings.problem: These stretches drop the close-third pressure for too long and turn the crew introduction into a casting parade. The chapter remains vivid, but the focalizer intermittently disappears from his own scene, which is exactly the screenplay drift the brief warns against.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread Silas's thought, bodily sensation, memory, or self-assessment into the blocks around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:91, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:219. The goal is not more exposition, but brief inward beats that keep Cora, Pru, Nico, and Vero filtered through his shame, pain, curiosity, or class recognition.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least two of the abstract glosses at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:405 are replaced by materially specific action or dialogue, and the shift from single authorship to shared infrastructure is still clear from the trays through the board sketch. Fail if the revised passage still explains the same single-name-versus-plural-use lesson in multiple abstract paraphrases after the scene has already shown it.
findings.evidence: The chapter states the same plurality lesson in successive abstract glosses at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:239, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:405, even after the tray sorting and label exchange have already dramatized it.
findings.problem: Silas's changed understanding is structurally sound, but the chapter keeps translating the scene back into thesis language instead of trusting the labor images. That repeated explanation blunts the discovery and makes the turn feel more taught than earned.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trim or convert at least two of the explanatory beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:405 into concrete action, object handling, or contested dialogue. Let the labeled trays, the shared charcoal, and the board layout carry the realization before Silas or the narrator paraphrases it.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if both cited lines are rewritten so the chapter trusts dialogue, labor rhythm, and object handling to signal the realization; fail if either moment still uses a self-announcing hinge.
findings.problem: Full-book explanatory hinge pattern ('There it was' / 'That landed') appears in 13 chapters. This chapter repeats it twice, at 5542 and 5860, when Junie's orders and Vero's challenge already give the scene enough force.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the hinge sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5542 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5860. Let Junie's dispatch, the labor crew's indifference, and Vero's direct challenge make the turn toward usefulness and the board idea legible without narrator signposting.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
